data_in.description: Spectrum vectors in low-dimensional representation (output from feature hashing stage)

domain_knowledge: Nearest neighbor indexes enable sublinear query time by partitioning the vector space, avoiding quadratic complexity of exhaustive all-versus-all spectrum comparisons.
Sparse distance matrices store only distances between spectra and their nearest neighbors, reducing memory footprint and enabling processing of millions of spectra.
The distance threshold parameter controls the sparsity of the output matrix—lower thresholds yield sparser matrices but risk missing related spectra.
Feature-hashed spectrum vectors enable efficient nearest neighbor searching while preserving cosine similarity relationships needed for MS/MS clustering.

direct_checks: verify that falcon repository at https://github.com/bittremieux/falcon contains implementation of nearest neighbor index querying logic in source files
verify file_exists: falcon source code contains a module or function responsible for sparse pairwise distance matrix computation using pre-built indexes

script_runs: execute falcon's nearest neighbor query stage on a test dataset of binned spectrum vectors (output from stage 1) and verify it produces a sparse distance matrix (CSR matrix, edge list, or equivalent sparse format) without exhaustive all-versus-all comparison

verify output_matches_reference: sparse matrix produced by querying stage contains only a subset of possible pairwise comparisons (row count and non-zero entry count significantly less than would result from dense all-versus-all comparison)
verify contains_substring: falcon source code or documentation contains explicit reference to 'nearest neighbor', 'index query', 'sparse', or 'distance matrix' in the module implementing this stage
expert_review: evaluate whether the sparse distance matrix computation correctly preserves spectrum similarity relationships necessary for downstream density-based clustering (no critical false negatives in neighbor detection)
assess algorithmic efficiency: confirm that the nearest neighbor index query approach reduces computational complexity compared to exhaustive all-versus-all pairwise distance calculation

review correctness of index construction and querying logic: verify that the implementation correctly uses the constructed nearest neighbor indexes to retrieve candidate neighbors and compute only those pairwise distances

cmd: import pickle; import numpy as np; from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix; vectors = pickle.load(open('{inputs.spectrum_vectors}', 'rb')); indexes = pickle.load(open('{inputs.nn_indexes}', 'rb')); rows, cols, data = [], [], []; [[[rows.append(i), cols.append(j), data.append(np.linalg.norm(vectors[i] - vectors[j]))] for j in indexes[i]] for i in range(len(vectors))]; sparse_matrix = csr_matrix((data, (rows, cols)), shape=(len(vectors), len(vectors))); pickle.dump(sparse_matrix, open('sparse_pairwise_distance_matrix.pkl', 'wb')); print(f'Sparse matrix: {sparse_matrix.shape}, sparsity: {1 - sparse_matrix.nnz / (sparse_matrix.shape[0] * sparse_matrix.shape[1]):.4f}')

finding: Nearest neighbor indexes are queried to compute a sparse pairwise distance matrix, avoiding exhaustive comparison of all spectra to each other.
inputs: Spectrum vectors in low-dimensional representation (output from feature hashing stage)
methodology_summary: Load spectrum vectors and nearest neighbor indexes from prior indexing stage.
Query each spectrum vector against nearest neighbor indexes to retrieve candidate matches within distance threshold.
Compute pairwise distances only between each spectrum and retrieved candidates.
Assemble distances into sparse matrix representation for memory efficiency.
Validation: Verify sparse matrix row and column dimensions match spectrum count; confirm sparsity (non-zero entry ratio) is substantially lower than dense all-versus-all matrix.

task_description: Query nearest neighbor indexes constructed from spectrum vectors to compute a sparse pairwise distance matrix for millions of MS/MS spectra without exhaustive all-versus-all comparison.

task_inputs.label: Low-dimensional spectrum vectors from feature hashing

task_objective: Implement the nearest neighbor index query stage that produces a sparse pairwise distance matrix for spectrum pairs without exhaustive all-versus-all comparison.
title: Reconstruct the Sparse Pairwise Distance Matrix Computation Layer of the falcon pipeline
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load pre-constructed nearest neighbor indexes and spectrum vector representations from the indexing stage. 2. Query each spectrum vector against the nearest neighbor indexes to retrieve candidate similar spectra within a user-defined distance threshold. 3. Compute pairwise distances only between each spectrum and its retrieved nearest neighbors, building a sparse distance matrix. 4. Export the sparse distance matrix in a format suitable for downstream density-based clustering.
